Thought 3: The narrative strongly points to Homer. The fingerprint is a crucial piece of evidence directly linking him to the scene. His training and ownership of blowguns directly connect him to the murder weapon. His admission about practicing "just a few days ago" (which the detective links to the time of the murder) is damning, especially since he has no alibi. His motive is also clear: Nathaniel was blackmailing him over his shady financial dealings. While Clara has a motive (the stolen necklace and the charity scam), her evidence is more circumstantial and emotional. The narrative's structure also heavily emphasizes the case against Homer, with the detective's "mind set" on him and the story ending with Winston leaving Homer "amidst the shadows." The evidence against Clara seems more like background information gathered during an interview, while the evidence against Homer is presented as the core of the case.
